title: rubus godronii var. macrobelophorus
intro: Rubus godronii var. macrobelophorus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a variety of raspberry plant native to the Himalayan region. It is a type of Rubus species, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. The leaves and fruits of Rubus godronii var. macrobelophorus can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to help support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health.
